executiveSummary:
NoType forked Template and evolved it along five axes that yield genuinely generic, reusable improvements worth porting back: (1) client-core transport/navigation/testing hardening, (2) server-core config/security/migration hardening, (3) a structurally-superset "Aurora" design system, (4) a complete generic Langfuse+OpenTelemetry observability layer, and (5) Apple/desktop platform + permissions infra. The four in-scope server FEATURE modules (auth/groups/files/privacy) are byte-identical after package normalization (verified via recursive diff + SHA), so there is essentially nothing to port there except a TLS-1.3 enforcement test. Likewise the client MVI feature cores are nearly identical; the real client deltas are in shared layers (core:navigation, core:sdk, core:storage, core:testing, app:designsystem) and the app shell.

The plan is ordered strictly by dependency: foundational build/env tooling first (EnvLoader, detekt hook, settings.json fix, version catalog), then shared-core hardening (SDK transport, ErrorMapper, navigation primitives, test fakes), then the design-system swap (tokens before effect-modifiers before components before screen rewiring), then server-core hardening, then a Koog 1.0 bump (hard prerequisite) followed by a new server:core:observability module and its wiring, then a new core:permissions + core:platform-macos module set plus desktop-shell capabilities and packaging, and finally docs/setup.sh and skill additions. I prefer many small, independently-verifiable phases over a few giant ones.

CRITICAL CROSS-CUTTING CONSTRAINT verified against source: Template keeps wasmJs as a first-class target; NoType dropped it. Therefore NoType's build.gradle.kts files must NEVER be copied wholesale — only additive deps are cherry-picked, Template's wasmJs blocks are kept, and every expect declaration ported from NoType MUST gain a wasmJs actual or commonMain will not compile. This is the single highest-frequency failure mode and is called out in nearly every phase.

VERIFICATION SPOT-CHECKS performed: Template's ApiClient has no HttpTimeout/WebSockets/requireSecureBaseUrl (NoType added all three); Template's ErrorMapper uses response.body<ErrorResponse>() and loses the body on parse failure (NoType captures raw body first); Template's core:navigation is just Route.kt (NoType added NavSelectionSignal/NavigateAdd/NavigationModule); Template's settings.json invokes the bash detekt hook via `node` (latent no-op bug NoType fixed to `bash`); Template Env.Ai is lean (enabled+googleApiKey); Template has no `auth-jwt` named JWT provider; Koog is 0.6.2 (NoType 1.0.0-preview7/beta-preview7 split); 21 Template feature-screen files reference Terminal* primitives (the design-swap surface); the design system is named "Aurora" everywhere in code (AuroraTheme/AuroraColors), whereas the user said "Aura" verbally — see designSystemDecision and openQuestions.
designSystemDecision:
RECOMMENDATION: Do a full REPLACE of Terminal with the Aurora system, but resolve the name first. There is a real naming conflict: the user said "Aura" verbally, but every symbol in NoType's code is "Aurora" (verified: AuroraTheme, AuroraColors, AuroraTypography, AuroraGlows, AuroraMotion, etc. — there are zero "Aura"-without-r symbols). Three options, in order of recommendation:

(A) PREFERRED — Adopt a NEUTRAL design-system name for the Template baseline (e.g. keep "Terminal" OR pick a brand-agnostic name like "Studio"/"Base"). Rationale: Template is a GENERIC starter; baking NoType's brand identity ("Aurora") into the template is itself a leak. Port the Aurora STRUCTURE (new token groups, expanded palette slots, 3-family typography, new components) into Template's existing Terminal system, keeping Terminal* names and supplying NEUTRAL default values (no neon trio as defaults, no midnight-canonical surfaces, single-font default for all three families unless fonts are added). This is the lowest-risk path and keeps Template brand-free.

(B) If the user truly wants the template rebranded to a real brand: name it "Aurora" (match the code, do NOT introduce "Aura" — that would mean renaming every NoType symbol for no benefit and would diverge from the verified codebase). Treat "Aura" as a verbal shorthand for "Aurora".

(C) Only choose "Aura" if the user explicitly wants a new, distinct brand name different from NoType's — then it is a global rename Aurora*->Aura* on top of the port.

SCOPE: The swap is large but mostly mechanical. Token data-class shapes are near-identical, so the theme API surface is forward-compatible — 21 Template feature-screen files call Terminal* primitives and need only a mechanical rename to re-skin automatically. Borders and Shadows token groups are byte-identical between systems — do NOT re-port them. Only Radius (adds xl=28dp slot), Colors (adds textFaint/bgElev2/borderStrong/fgOnNeon + neon accent slots), and Typography (3 families + semantic scale, with legacy names re-pointed for back-compat) actually changed; Glows and Motion are net-new groups.

MIGRATION ORDER (must be respected or ports won't compile): (1) token groups first — add Motion (drop-in), Glows (structure + neutral defaults), then Color slots (textFaint/bgElev2/borderStrong/fgOnNeon + accent slots) and the Radius xl slot, then the 3-family Typography KEEPING the legacy-name aliasing trick (load-bearing — it's what makes the swap non-breaking); (2) effect modifiers (auroraBorder/selectedAccent/PulseDot/auroraBrush, generalized to take theme colors — these depend on the Glows group); (3) new generic components (SegmentedControl, RangeSlider, ModalSheet, ChipsInput, KbdCombo, NavRail, DiffEventRow, ModeBadge — each depends on the token slots it references); (4) finally the 1:1 Terminal*->chosen-name rename across the 21 feature screens, after which they re-skin automatically.

EXCLUDE (product brand/voice): RecordingHudPill, HudPillSurface, RecordingPillWave, AuroraWaveform, AuroraBrandWaveform, HudIcons, AuroraBrandLogo, the neon trio as DEFAULT values, midnight #07080C as canonical surface, Space Grotesk/Manrope as DEFAULT fonts, the notype-design skill, and aurora-design-request.md. Keep the SLOTS/structure, not these literal brand values.

DESIGN-SKILL CONSEQUENCE: If the design system is renamed/replaced, Template's terminal-design-generator/implementer/sync skill trio + terminal_design_system.pen + manifest.json/REFERENCES.md/CODE-MAP.md and the CLAUDE.md "Design <-> Code Mapping (Pencil)" section all become stale and must be re-authored for the new token set, OR the swap must update them in lockstep. This is a non-trivial sub-project (see openQuestions and Phase D5).
openQuestions: DESIGN-SYSTEM NAME: You said 'Aura' but the NoType code says 'Aurora' everywhere (verified: no 'Aura'-without-r symbols exist). Pick one: (A) keep a NEUTRAL/Terminal name and only port Aurora's structure with brand-free defaults [recommended for a generic template]; (B) rebrand the template to 'Aurora' to match the code; or (C) introduce a genuinely new 'Aura' brand requiring a global Aurora*->Aura* rename. This decision gates the entire design-system phase set and the Pencil-skill re-authoring.

REBRAND INTENT: Is the goal to harvest Aurora's GENERIC improvements into Template's existing Terminal system (keep Terminal naming, neutral defaults) OR to literally replace Terminal with the Aurora look-and-feel (neon palette, dark-canonical surfaces, Space Grotesk/Manrope fonts)? The former is brand-free and low-risk; the latter bakes NoType's identity into the 'generic' template.

FONTS: Adopting the 3-family typography either (a) ships Space Grotesk + Manrope .ttf assets into Template's composeResources/font, or (b) keeps Template's single JetBrains Mono font as the default for all three families. Which? (a) makes the template look like NoType; (b) keeps the structure generic.

REQUEST TIMEOUT DEFAULT: NoType sets requestTimeoutMillis=INFINITE (deliberate, for long LLM bodies). For a generic template, do you want a sane finite default (e.g. 30s) with connect/request timeouts exposed as constructor params, or mirror INFINITE? Recommended: finite default + params.

KOOG BUMP SCOPE: Porting the observability layer requires bumping Koog from 0.6.2 to the 1.0.0-preview7 line (an ALPHA/preview, with two non-binary-compatible streams). Do you want the generic template baseline to depend on a preview Koog, or should the observability module be optional/opt-in (shipped but not wired into the default server) so the template baseline stays on stable Koog until 1.0 GA?

OBSERVABILITY DEPTH: Should Template ship the FULL observability stack (OTel SDK + SpanAdapter + Langfuse REST clients + prompt-provider + eval/judge harness + self-hosted Langfuse docker profile) or only the lower tiers (OTel SDK + SpanAdapter + Env fields), deferring prompt-management/eval to when a project actually adds an LLM feature?

COMPOSE HOT RELOAD BUMP: The compose-hot-reload MCP server needs the :composeApp:hotMcpServerJvm task, which likely requires bumping composeHotReload 1.0.0 -> 1.2.0-alpha01 (an alpha) in the template baseline. Accept an alpha plugin in the generic baseline, or hold until a stable release?

CROSS-PLATFORM CI: Several ported dev/CI scripts (server-watch.sh, check-binary-size.sh, check-idle-rss.sh, run-dev-bundle.sh) are macOS-only (BSD stat -f, ps -o rss=, dmg/.app paths). If Template's CI runs on Linux, do you want Linux fallback branches added, or are these acceptable as macOS-only dev helpers?

NEW MODULES: Approve introducing new shared modules — server:core:observability, core:permissions, core:platform-macos (or core:native), core:securestorage — and promoting InMemorySettings into core:testing? These are the right homes for the generic infra but add to the module graph.

PERMISSIONS GENERALIZATION: Approve generalizing AppError.Audio.PermissionDenied -> AppError.Permission.Denied(permission) and adding a generic Permission enum (Microphone/Camera/Accessibility/Notifications/Location) + PermissionController expect/actual in core:models/core:permissions? This is the clean generic shape but touches core:models.
globalRisks: wasmJs regression (HIGHEST FREQUENCY): Template keeps wasmJs as a first-class target; NoType dropped it. NEVER copy NoType build.gradle.kts files wholesale (they delete the wasmJs target and the kotlin-js-store/wasm/yarn.lock). Cherry-pick only additive deps and KEEP Template's wasmJs blocks. Every expect declaration ported from NoType (rememberSystemDarkTheme, allAppModules, sharedModule, permission seams, reduced-motion, cross-feature content slots) MUST gain a wasmJs actual or commonMain will not compile.
Package rename: every ported file is com.m2f.notype.*; rewrite to com.m2f.template.* (and rootProject 'notype'->'template' coordinates). Easy to miss in copied files.

EnvLoader ordering: copy EnvLoader.kt into Template/buildSrc BEFORE adding any :server:run / run-task doFirst block that references it, or the build script won't compile. EnvLoader is plain Kotlin (no buildSrc/build.gradle.kts change needed).

detekt hook is inert without the settings.json fix: Template invokes the bash hook via `node` (verified) which silently does nothing. The hook rewrite REQUIRES flipping that command to `bash` in the same change, else the upgrade does not run. DETEKT_VERSION is hardcoded (1.23.8) — keep it in sync with libs.versions.toml.

Design-system token-before-component ordering: Glows must land before auroraGlow/selectedAccent/SegmentedControl; textFaint before KbdCombo; bgElev2/borderStrong/fgOnNeon before any component referencing them; the legacy-typography aliasing trick must be preserved or it becomes a breaking change across all 21 screens. Borders/Shadows are byte-identical — do not re-port.

Koog 1.0 is a hard prerequisite for the ENTIRE observability tracing stack — none of the SpanAdapter/OTel-SDK code compiles on 0.6.2. The two Koog streams (core preview7 vs ext beta-preview7) are NOT binary-compatible; pin both and do not mix. Koog OTel API is preview/unstable.

Observability is a single interlocking fix: process-lifetime SDK + NonClosingOpenTelemetry + simple (not batch) span processor + setShutdownOnAgentClose(true), plus two verified self-hosted-Langfuse-v3 gotchas (trace.output must be set on the INFERENCE span not just the root; promptVersion must go through freeform metadata not the reserved int field). Porting piecemeal reintroduces flat traces or per-call leaks. Reconcile with the existing koog-langfuse-tracing / langfuse-prompt-management skills — do not fork a third copy of the adapter.

Module placement: server:core:observability must depend on server:core:config, not the reverse; keep server:core:config dependency-light (only gains Langfuse Env fields + LangfuseConfig value type). The new secret-storage backend belongs in a new core:securestorage, NOT lifted from core:audio with its AES audio-key logic.

core:models coupling for permissions: the gates/probes/deep-links return Either<AppError,_> using AppError.Native.* and AppError.Audio.PermissionDenied. Define generic AppError.Permission.* / AppError.Native.* in Template's core:models BEFORE porting the seams, or they won't compile. Prefer generalizing to AppError.Permission.Denied(permission) during the port.

macOS entitlements footgun: a custom entitlementsFile REPLACES Compose Desktop's default wholesale — if you port entitlements.plist you MUST keep the three JVM hardened-runtime keys (allow-jit, allow-unsigned-executable-memory, disable-library-validation) or the bundled JVM won't launch. Strip the mic-specific keys, keep the JVM keys.

Do NOT port NoType's NoType-local regressions: the workflow_dispatch-only CI triggers (a billing block, keep Template's push/PR triggers), the wasmJs/ktor-client-js removal, the macos-native-gate.yml, and the Aurora*->Terminal* rename leaking into shared skills/code-reviewer.md (port only the verification paragraph, discard the rename).

Directionality: Template has MORE RECENT commits than NoType on some server features (quick-7 inline export, quick-9 password verification) and the password->token rename is already consistent in BOTH repos — confirm Template is the up-to-date baseline before assuming a delta, and do not re-apply the token rename.

InMemorySettings is duplicated per-module in NoType — promote ONCE to core:testing rather than copying per feature. Also verify whether multiplatform-settings-test/MapSettings is published for Template's target set before adopting the hand-rolled fake.

Several reusable seams (permission/accessibility/reduced-motion) live inside the hidden app:dictation:impl module in NoType; on port they must move UP into shared core modules (core:permissions, core:platform-macos, app:designsystem) so other features can consume them — don't replicate the impl-hidden placement.

Notarization is only partially present in NoType (hardened-runtime entitlements + dev re-sign script + asc-* skills, but NO end-to-end notarize/staple Gradle task). If the template promises 'notarization resolved', that last mile still needs authoring; the entitlements/signing groundwork is a starting point, not a complete pipeline.

phases.phase: P0 — Build/env foundation: EnvLoader + detekt hook + settings.json fix + version catalog + gitignore
phases.goal: Land the zero-coupling build tooling that everything else benefits from, with no behavioral risk to wasmJs or the module graph.
workItems: Copy buildSrc/src/main/kotlin/EnvLoader.kt verbatim into Template/buildSrc/src/main/kotlin/ (plain Kotlin, no buildSrc/build.gradle.kts change). Source: /Users/marc/IdeaProjects/NoType/buildSrc/src/main/kotlin/EnvLoader.kt

Rewrite Template/.claude/hooks/detekt-on-edit.sh from NoType's version (standalone detekt CLI per-edited-file, two passes, cached jars, nearest baseline). Source: /Users/marc/IdeaProjects/NoType/.claude/hooks/detekt-on-edit.sh. Keep DETEKT_VERSION in sync with Template libs.versions.toml (1.23.8).

Fix Template/.claude/settings.json PostToolUse detekt command from `node .claude/hooks/detekt-on-edit.sh` to `bash .claude/hooks/detekt-on-edit.sh` (verified latent bug). Source: /Users/marc/IdeaProjects/NoType/.claude/settings.json

Add gitignore globs: hs_err_pid*.log, replay_pid*.log, .claude/hooks/.detekt/. Do NOT remove /kotlin-js-store/wasm/yarn.lock (Template keeps wasmJs). Source: /Users/marc/IdeaProjects/NoType/.gitignore

Add version-catalog aliases ktor-client-mock and ktor-client-websockets (version.ref=ktor, already 3.4.0). Do NOT remove ktor-client-js. Source: /Users/marc/IdeaProjects/NoType/gradle/libs.versions.toml
phases.verification: ./gradlew help (build scripts still compile with EnvLoader present); edit a .kt file and confirm the detekt hook runs (non-blocking, ~1-2s) via `bash .claude/hooks/detekt-on-edit.sh <file>`; ./gradlew detekt still passes.

phases.phase: P1 — Server :server:run .env injection (EnvLoader wiring) + Env.kt robust discovery
phases.goal: Make the committed .env authoritative over ambient shell vars for local :server:run, and fix Gradle CWD not seeing the root .env (12-factor precedence).
workItems: Add the ~13-line `tasks.named<JavaExec>("run") { doFirst { EnvLoader.load(rootProject.projectDir).forEach { (k,v) -> environment(k,v) } } }` block to Template/server/build.gradle.kts (execution-time doFirst, config-cache safe). Do NOT port the adjacent product `implementation(projects.server.transcripts.wire)` line. Genericize the LANGFUSE_* example comment. Source: /Users/marc/IdeaProjects/NoType/server/build.gradle.kts

Port ONLY the dotenvDirectory walk-up block + .directory(dotenvDirectory) + the env() change to System.getenv(key) ?: dotenv[key] in Env.kt. Do NOT port any Ai/Stt/Langfuse/ZDR/cap fields. Source: /Users/marc/IdeaProjects/NoType/server/core/config/src/main/kotlin/com/m2f/core/config/configuration/Env.kt

Port EnvTest's dotenv-fixture recipe (temp .env round-trip) retargeted to a generic key (NoType asserts DEEPGRAM_* — rename). Source: /Users/marc/IdeaProjects/NoType/server/core/config/src/test/kotlin/com/m2f/core/config/EnvTest.kt
phases.verification: ./gradlew :server:run with a project-root .env containing a test var and the same var exported in the shell to a different value — confirm the .env value wins inside the JVM; ./gradlew :server:core:config:test passes the new EnvTest.

phases.phase: P2 — core:sdk transport hardening (HttpTimeout + WebSockets + requireSecureBaseUrl)
phases.goal: Bound the connect phase, add a WebSockets client plugin for future streaming, and refuse cleartext non-loopback baseUrls — generic transport-security and resilience baseline.
workItems: Add `implementation(libs.ktor.client.websockets)` to Template core:sdk commonMain (additive only — KEEP Template's wasmJs blocks; do NOT copy NoType's build.gradle wholesale). Source delta: /Users/marc/IdeaProjects/NoType/core/sdk/build.gradle.kts

In createApiClient: install(HttpTimeout){ connectTimeoutMillis = CONNECT_TIMEOUT_MS; requestTimeoutMillis = <finite default, e.g. 30s> } with both exposed as constructor params (do NOT hardcode INFINITE — that's a NoType product call). Source: /Users/marc/IdeaProjects/NoType/core/sdk/src/commonMain/kotlin/com/m2f/notype/sdk/ApiClient.kt
install(WebSockets){ pingIntervalMillis = ... } on the shared client.
Copy requireSecureBaseUrl + LOOPBACK_HOSTS verbatim, call at top of createApiClient, strip audio/transcript wording from the doc comment. Same source file.
phases.verification: ./gradlew :core:sdk:allTests (all targets incl. wasmJs compile/link); add a quick unit test that requireSecureBaseUrl("http://example.com") throws and ("http://localhost:8080") / ("http://10.0.2.2") pass.

phases.phase: P3 — core:sdk ErrorMapper raw-body capture + body-code dispatch hook
phases.goal: Stop discarding error bodies that don't match the {code,message} ErrorResponse shape; add a generic (no-op default) body-code dispatch extension point.
workItems: Rewrite mapHttpError to read response.bodyAsText() ONCE, decode ErrorResponse from that string with ignoreUnknownKeys, preserve the raw body (BODY_PREVIEW_CHARS-truncated) in the AppError.Client.Unknown fallback. Source: /Users/marc/IdeaProjects/NoType/core/sdk/src/commonMain/kotlin/com/m2f/notype/sdk/ErrorMapper.kt

Add the body-code dispatch as a GENERIC no-op/pluggable hook (errorResponse?.let { domainCodeMapper(it)?.let { return it } }). DROP mapAiErrorCode/mapCleanupStreamErrorCode (AI_/AUDIO_ codes are product-specific). apiCall()/mapException() unchanged.
Verified baseline: Template currently does response.body<ErrorResponse>() and loses the body on parse failure.
phases.verification: ./gradlew :core:sdk:allTests; add a unit test (MockEngine) returning a non-ErrorResponse-shaped 400 body and assert the raw body is preserved in AppError.Client.Unknown.detail.

phases.phase: P4 — core:navigation primitives (NavSelectionSignal + navigateAdd + navigationModule)
phases.goal: Add the reusable cross-feature signalling + append-only back-stack toolkit Template lacks (Template's core:navigation is just Route.kt — verified).
workItems: Add `implementation(libs.koin.core)` + `implementation(libs.kotlinx.coroutines.core)` to Template core:navigation commonMain (additive; KEEP wasmJs target).

Copy NavSelectionSignal.kt (+ its commonTest NavSelectionSignalTest.kt), NavigateAdd.kt, NavigationModule.kt; rename package; genericize KDocs (strip DashboardRoute/tray/WR-04/macOS-AX references, keep the WHY a route push can't swap a pane). Keep NavSelectionSignal STRING-typed (do not 'improve' to an enum — that reintroduces the feature cycle). Sources under /Users/marc/IdeaProjects/NoType/core/navigation/src/commonMain/kotlin/com/m2f/notype/navigation/
Wire navigationModule into the shared DI aggregator (sharedModule) as a plain val include (do NOT convert sharedModule to expect/actual just for this).
Port these ONCE — they overlap across the navigation, app-features, and app-shell findings.

Default colors to theme accents, not the neon trio. Sources: /Users/marc/IdeaProjects/NoType/app/designsystem/.../modifier/AuroraEffects.kt + AuroraSelection.kt

Components (rename + generalize accent/brand bits): SegmentedControl, RangeSlider, ModalSheet, ChipsInput, KbdCombo (needs textFaint), NavRail (make logo slot + items caller-supplied), DiffEventRow, ModeBadge (generalize Cloud/OnDevice variants).
